other_observers: Kent Nickell, Bill and Susan Miller, Phil Cafaro, Marcia Maeda; I am the original finder and identifier
specific_location: Boyd Lake State Park Swim Beach
description: Gull, smaller than Ring-billed Gull, mostly white head with dark gray smudge behind eye extending to nape. Narrow, longish black bill. Upperparts gray (about the shade of California Gull) with white scapular and tertial crescents, and jet black wing tips (at rest). Underparts mostly white with pale gray wash to sides of upper breast. Legs black. Wings with long extension 1-2 inches beyond tail. In flight, wings long and pointed; slow wingbeats made the bird appear like a large gull in flight; Secondaries of wings and their coverts gray blending into all black primaries and primary coverts. Underwing appeared dark in flight (at a distance of several hundred feet). Trailing edge of wing white.Behaviors: Walking along beach scavenging for food, flying over water and sitting on waterCall: nonePlumage: sub adult 2nd cycle
photographer: Photographer: Nick Komar, Nick Komar, Nick Komar, Nick Komar
